filings.summary: Material agreement signed; Bylaw / charter change
filings.ai_summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. issued 75 million depositary shares representing mandatory convertible preferred stock on June 10, 2026.

filings.summary: Material agreement signed; Other material update
filings.ai_summary: Super Micro Computer entered agreements to sell up to $1.25 billion in stock and amended its credit agreement.

Super Micro is selling 75 million $50 depositary shares of 7% mandatory‑convertible preferred stock, raising about $3.68 billion to fund AI‑server component purchases, with automatic conversion to common shares by June 2029.

Super Micro Computer entered an agreement with JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs and Citi to sell up to $1.25 billion of its common stock, using the proceeds to fund AI‑server component purchases and other corporate needs.

Super Micro is selling 45.45 million common shares at $27.50 each, expecting about $1.22 billion in net proceeds to fund AI‑server component purchases and other corporate needs, alongside a separate depositary‑share offering and a $1.25 billion ATM program.
Super Micro Computer, Inc. is offering 75 million depositary shares representing interests in mandatory convertible preferred stock to fund AI server component purchases.
Super Micro Computer, Inc. is offering $1.25 billion of common stock to fund component purchases for AI servers.
Super Micro Computer updated its risk factor disclosures in an SEC filing on June 9, 2026.
